sections.title: Gathering Powers
sections.description: Test @Focus to gather a power. If you succeed, you can gather a power card from your power deck and keep this card until your next @NightsRest.
sections.mechanics: Roll 1d10 against @Focus. If the roll is equal to or less than your @Focus score, gather one power from your deck. If you fail, take 1 damage and discard your current cards
sections.limits: You can only have 3 powers gathered at one time. If you want to discard a power to replace it, you must discard all in your hand.
sections.recovery: When you @Gritteeth, you automatically gather 3 powers without needing to test @Focus.

sections.title: Using Powers
sections.description: You can play power cards at any time, as long as any conditions stated in the card's description are fulfilled. Follow the instructions on the power card.
sections.timing: Powers have no action cost — they can be played on your turn, on another hunter's turn, or even in response to a monster's action, so long as the card's conditions are met.
sections.targeting: You cannot target something with a power if you cannot perceive it, although you might still be able to harm it. You cannot select a target without a clear unbroken line from you to the location of it.
sections.recovery: Discarded powers can be returned to your power deck when you complete a @NightsRest.

sections.title: Power Rarities
sections.description: Every power card belongs to one of four rarity tiers. Rarity indicates how dramatically a power can shift the course of a fight — from a single targeted effect to something capable of ending an encounter entirely.
sections.charge: To play a @Rare or @Mythic power, at least one @Common or @Uncommon card must first be played face-down from your hand as a charge. A charged card provides no effect of its own — it is spent to fuel the more powerful card. The charge is discarded alongside the @Rare or @Mythic card when it resolves.

rarities.name: Common
rarities.description: A focused, immediate effect against a single target within arm's reach.

rarities.name: Uncommon
rarities.description: A broader effect hitting one to three targets. May last beyond the moment it's played, persisting until a discard condition on the card is triggered.
rarities.duration: Instant, or until the card's discard condition is met

rarities.name: Rare
rarities.description: A powerful effect that can sweep across a large area or many creatures at once. Requires at least one @Common or @Uncommon card played face-down as a charge before it can be used.

rarities.name: Mythic
rarities.description: The rarest and most devastating powers. A @Mythic card, once played, can singlehandedly end an encounter by causing fatal effects against its target. Requires at least one @Common or @Uncommon card played face-down as a charge before it can be used.

sections.title: Card Rarity & Deck Building
sections.description: Power cards come in four levels of rarity: Common, Uncommon, Rare, and Mythic. When building a deck, you must follow these rarity requirements:
requirements: For every Mythic card, you must include at least 2 Rare cards
For every Rare card, you must include at least 2 Uncommon cards
For every Uncommon card, you must include at least 2 Common cards
